definition: Fabricated or hallucinated citations, authorities, or references enter legal, administrative, expert, or scholarly records.
minimum_public_evidence_for_T1: One public source describing the contaminated filing, submission, report, or paper and identifying the citation problem.
authoritative_upgrade_path_for_T3: Court order, tribunal record, regulator notice, journal/editor notice, retraction, or institutional investigation confirming the contamination.
preferred_source_families: Court and tribunal records; Regulator and enforcement sources; Academic and research-integrity sources; News and investigative reporting
paper_or_thesis_use: Supports a methods/results table showing that citation fabrication is a reusable edge-case pattern across legal and academic settings.
visualization_hook: Subtype-by-evidence-tier heatmap; claim-support matrix for hostile-reviewer pass
caution_or_limit: Do not infer intent, unlawfulness, or professional misconduct without an authoritative source.

definition: Generated or synthetic expert material, witness-style reports, or evidentiary submissions are used without clear provenance or validation.
minimum_public_evidence_for_T1: One public source describing the submission/use of AI-generated expert-style material in a consequential process.
authoritative_upgrade_path_for_T3: Court ruling, regulator action, institutional policy notice, or official investigation documenting the provenance or admissibility issue.
preferred_source_families: Court and tribunal records; Regulator and enforcement sources; Company and platform notices; News and investigative reporting
paper_or_thesis_use: Useful for a paper section on evidentiary provenance confusion and bounded attestation claims.
visualization_hook: Evidence-source Sankey linking subtype to authority family and downstream harm
caution_or_limit: Public reporting may overstate system capability; separate source allegation from adjudicated finding.

definition: Synthetic audio, image, or video contaminates a filing, hearing, investigation, or adjudicative record.
minimum_public_evidence_for_T1: One public source describing the media item and the procedural setting it affected.
authoritative_upgrade_path_for_T3: Court finding, police or regulator notice, forensic report, or official case summary confirming synthetic-media relevance.
preferred_source_families: Court and tribunal records; Regulator and enforcement sources; Security research and advisories; News and investigative reporting
paper_or_thesis_use: Feeds a cross-domain figure connecting provenance confusion to legal/process harm without claiming media-authenticity certainty.
visualization_hook: Timeline or topology linking media provenance disputes to process stage
caution_or_limit: Metadata-only treatment is preferred where harmful media would otherwise be mirrored.

definition: Automated or AI-assisted filings, complaints, or submissions overload or distort administrative or judicial procedure.
minimum_public_evidence_for_T1: One public source describing the automated submission behavior and its procedural effect.
authoritative_upgrade_path_for_T3: Court sanctions order, agency notice, platform abuse report, or official administrative response.
preferred_source_families: Court and tribunal records; Regulator and enforcement sources; Company and platform notices; News and investigative reporting
paper_or_thesis_use: Can support a paper paragraph on procedural overload as a governance-edge case distinct from classic misinformation.
visualization_hook: Process-funnel figure showing submission stage, screening stage, and intervention stage
caution_or_limit: Absent authoritative records, keep legal status and scale claims conservative.

definition: Synthetic or AI-assisted peer review, benchmark reporting, or dataset description introduces false claims about evaluation or review.
minimum_public_evidence_for_T1: One public source describing the integrity concern in review, evaluation, or dataset documentation.
authoritative_upgrade_path_for_T3: Journal/editor notice, conference organizer statement, institutional investigation, or peer-reviewed methods critique.
preferred_source_families: Academic and research-integrity sources; Company and platform notices; News and investigative reporting
paper_or_thesis_use: Directly supports LIMEN's residual-case claim by showing research-integrity incidents that do not fit standard safety or bias taxonomies.
visualization_hook: Matrix of research-integrity subtype x authority source x claim strength
caution_or_limit: Do not classify a paper or dataset as fraudulent from media coverage alone.

definition: A system, paper, benchmark, or service falsely claims AI autonomy, AI provenance, or AI-generated output quality while hiding human substitution or fabrication.
minimum_public_evidence_for_T1: One public source documenting the false-AI allegation or deceptive provenance issue.
authoritative_upgrade_path_for_T3: Regulator notice, journal correction, company admission, procurement record, or authoritative investigative finding.
preferred_source_families: Regulator and enforcement sources; Academic and research-integrity sources; Public-sector registers and procurement; News and investigative reporting
paper_or_thesis_use: Supports comparative discussion between research-integrity failures and AI-washing cases in the atlas.
visualization_hook: Comparative bar chart of false-AI provenance vs citation fabrication pathways
caution_or_limit: Keep allegations about deception or fraud provisional unless an authoritative source states them.

definition: Benchmark gaming, evaluation leakage, or synthetic evidence contamination undermines public claims about model capability or safety.
minimum_public_evidence_for_T1: One public source describing the benchmark or evidence contamination mechanism at a non-dangerous summary level.
authoritative_upgrade_path_for_T3: Peer-reviewed paper, official benchmark maintainer notice, institutional report, or security advisory.
preferred_source_families: Academic and research-integrity sources; Security research and advisories; Company and platform notices
paper_or_thesis_use: Useful for a methods note on why LIMEN separates attestation of processing from truth of capability claims.
visualization_hook: Method-note figure linking contamination type to evidence-tier ceiling
caution_or_limit: Avoid operational reproduction details; record only public-safe summaries.

definition: A case materially affects research or evidentiary integrity but resists existing database categories, requiring residual tracking for later taxonomy revision.
minimum_public_evidence_for_T1: One public source that clearly describes the edge case and why standard categories fit poorly.
authoritative_upgrade_path_for_T3: Any authoritative confirmation relevant to the setting, or multiple independent public sources if no authority exists.
preferred_source_families: Academic and research-integrity sources; Court and tribunal records; Regulator and enforcement sources; News and investigative reporting
paper_or_thesis_use: Creates a bounded queue for taxonomy refinement and demonstrates why LIMEN needs an edge-case residual bucket.
visualization_hook: Residual-cluster table for future taxonomy proposals
caution_or_limit: Residual status is a methodological signal, not a claim that the case is more important than classified ones.
